title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Execution Trace: Feed Go Identity Precedence
summary: RSS and Atom transport structs leak into storage, missing data is invented, HTML and text are conflated, source identifiers are discarded, and updates cannot be explained. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go identity precedence increment in loss-aware normalized feed and entry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Choose entry identity from stable Atom ID, RSS GUID semantics, canonical link, or scoped content fingerprint with explicit precedence.
Convert format-specific transport values into validated feed, entry, person, link, category, content, and provenance domain types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Identity Precedence · Theory Feed Go Identity Precedence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 1744 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
